title: 211. Add and Search Word - Data structure design
question:
Design a data structure that supports the following two operations:

void addWord(word)
bool search(word)

search(word) can search a literal word or a regular expression string containing only letters a-z or .. A . means it can represent any one letter.

Example:

addWord("bad")
addWord("dad")
addWord("mad")
search("pad") -> false
search("bad") -> true
search(".ad") -> true
search("b..") -> true

Note:
You may assume that all words are consist of lowercase letters a-z.
code: class WordDictionary(object):\u000A\u000A    def __init__(self):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        Initialize your data structure here.\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        self.trie_tree \u003D {}\u000A\u000A    def addWord(self, word):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        Adds a word into the data structure.\u000A        :type word: str\u000A        :rtype: void\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        cur \u003D self.trie_tree\u000A        for char in word:\u000A            cur \u003D cur.setdefault(char, {})\u000A        cur[True] \u003D word\u000A\u000A    def search(self, word):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        Returns if the word is in the data structure. A word could contain the dot character \u0027.\u0027 to represent any one letter.\u000A        :type word: str\u000A        :rtype: bool\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        stack \u003D [self.trie_tree]\u000A        for char in word:\u000A            if not stack: return False\u000A            if char \u003D\u003D \u0027.\u0027:\u000A                stack \u003D [cur[cur_char] for cur in stack for cur_char in cur if cur_char !\u003D True]\u000A            else:\u000A                stack \u003D [cur[char] for cur in stack if char in cur]\u000A        return any(cur[True] for cur in stack if True in cur)\u000A\u000A\u000A# Your WordDictionary object will be instantiated and called as such:\u000A# obj \u003D WordDictionary()\u000A# obj.addWord(word)\u000A# param_2 \u003D obj.search(word)
